steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The equation $5m = 4(n + p)$ has $m$ multiplied by 5. To isolate $m$, divide both sides by 5.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• "$m = \frac{4(n+p)}{5}$": Exactly the result of dividing both sides by 5. ✓
  • "$m = 4(n + p)$": Omits the division by 5; this would require the original equation to be $m = 4(n+p)$. ✗
  • "$m = 4(n + p)−5$": Incorrectly subtracts 5 instead of dividing by 5. ✗
  • "$m = 4 − n − p − 5$": Incorrectly distributes and subtracts rather than divides by 5. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
